text.title: Ambiguity and Contradiction Detector Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Apply to a single requirements document at a time. Never blend findings across multiple docs into one report.
rule.rationale: Cross-doc batching loses the per-doc remediation owner; each doc gets its own report.

rule.statement: Detector runs against a closed checklist of categories (vague_quantifier / undefined_term / contradictory_ac / missing_edge_case / unowned_decision). 'Other' category is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Open-ended detection diverges per operator; closed checklist makes findings comparable across reviews.

rule.statement: Every finding cites the exact quote from the doc (line/section reference + verbatim text). Findings without evidence quote rejected.
rule.rationale: Without verbatim evidence, finding is opinion. Quotes let the requirements owner reproduce the issue and decide remediation.

rule.statement: Every finding has severity (blocking / major / minor) AND remediation owner.
rule.rationale: Without severity, all findings get equal treatment; without owner, none are closed.

rule.statement: Output report carries semver version + last_reviewed + run_id. Stale reports (>30 days, no re-run) flagged on read.
rule.rationale: Requirements drift; report dated 6 months ago anchors to obsolete state.

rule.statement: Skip for ≤1-page memo OR research spike OR doc already passed prior detector run within 7 days.
rule.rationale: Methodology overhead exceeds value on tiny docs; recent runs don't need re-run.
